behavior_summary: Implements a multi-stage "sinking" stepping-stone tile: when an actor enters its fast/trigger area it begins a timed sink animation that changes tile frames/types, plays SFX, spawns visual/effect helpers, and moves items between z-layers (lifting some up and letting others fall).

Provides area-entry/exit and release handlers: enterFastArea spawns the main sink controller; leaveFastArea forces a stable type and runs cleanup effects; release can grant a small dexterity reward when the tile surface weight is zero.

Three subordinate helper processes (01D6, 0341, 04D3) coordinate the visual/phased animation and physically move items via Item::push/Item::pop, getEtherealTop, and Item::fall. FREE::* effect spawns and playSFX calls accompany the animation.
notable_control_flow: Area entry (U8P_SINKING::enterFastArea) spawns the central controller U8P_SINKING::0114 rather than making immediate atomic changes — behaviour is implemented as inter-process sequencing.

U8P_SINKING::0114: read item quality and frame, spawn scaled FREE::1C19 effects and subordinate helpers (U8P_SINKING::01D6, U8P_SINKING::04D3, U8P_SINKING::0341), toggle frames/types, play SFX, and use repeated spawn/imply/suspend (yields) to time multi-step animation.

Helpers perform push/pop iterations over the tile stack and use getEtherealTop to compute intermediary z positions — items are selectively popped, moved or sent to fall state across several timed yields.
leaveFastArea performs a forced type change (cleanup) and spawns FREE::2A56 for visual cleanup; release conditionally calls AccumulateDexterity when surface weight == 0.
open_questions: Exact semantics and numeric meanings of the type/frame constants used (examples observed: 0x79, 0x015B, 0x020F) and whether they map to specific in-game tiles/animations.
Precise PID semantics for some calls (e.g., whether the second parameter to spawn is always the actor who triggered the sink or sometimes a different process id).
Whether release is triggered only when an actor steps off the tile or also when other objects are removed — the trigger condition is inferred but not explicit.
Edge-case behavior for stacked containers: how nested container contents are selected/filtered during push/pop scans (the selection predicate is inferred from disassembly patterns).

main_function.name: U8P_SINKING::look
main_function.purpose: Handle a player/actor 'U8P_SINKING::look' (examine) action on the sinking tile/item: set per-instance info/exclude and invoke Item::bark to display the descriptive string "stepping stone ". Does not alter world state beyond per-instance flags.
entry_conditions: Event 0: invoked when an actor inspects/looks at the item (U8P_SINKING::look/examine event)

function_map.name: U8P_SINKING::look
function_map.role: Examine/tooltip handler that invokes Item::bark with "stepping stone " and sets per-instance info/process exclusion

function_map.name: U8P_SINKING::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Area-entry trigger: sets instance info/process-exclude and spawns U8P_SINKING::0114 to start the sink sequence

function_map.name: U8P_SINKING::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Area-exit trigger: sets instance info/process-exclude, forces item type to a stable type (reset) and spawns cleanup effect FREE::2A56

function_map.name: U8P_SINKING::release
function_map.role: Release handler: sets instance info/process-exclude then checks Item::getSurfaceWeight; if zero, calls AccumulateDexterity(0x0F)

function_map.name: U8P_SINKING::0114
function_map.role: Primary sinking controller: sequences animation frames/types, plays SFX, spawns subordinate handlers (01D6, 04D3, 0341), and spawns FREE::1C19 effects scaled by Item::getQuality. Uses suspend/yield timing.

function_map.name: U8P_SINKING::01D6
function_map.role: Upward-lift phase: set visual type/frame, push/pop tile stack at z+offset to lift items, scan container contents and spawn a small FREE::1C19 effect (code 8)

function_map.name: U8P_SINKING::0341
function_map.role: Complementary phase: set type/frame, iterate stack to move items via ethereal top adjustments and spawn FREE::1C19 (code 5); coordinates downward/transfer movement

function_map.name: U8P_SINKING::04D3
function_map.role: Content-release routine: change tile type/frame and for qualifying stack entries use getEtherealTop + Item::pop + Item::fall to cause items to drop/fall

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn the primary sink controller to run the timed sinking sequence for this item/pid
Spawn the upward-lift helper phase as part of the multi-step animation
Spawn the content-release helper that triggers Item::fall for affected items
Spawn a complementary phase helper to move items via ethereal top adjustments and continue animation sequencing

external_calls.purpose: Display the descriptive string "stepping stone " to the inspecting actor/process
Record per-instance info and exclude the spawned/affected process from global lists (engine idiom to manage instance state)
Force the item's visual/behavior type to a fixed type (reset on exit)
Spawn cleanup/free effect when leaving the trigger area
Query surface weight to decide whether to award AccumulateDexterity
Grant a small dexterity increase (0x0F) when surface weight == 0
Scale the number/strength of spawned FREE::1C19 effects based on item quality
Play sound effects at the tile to accompany sink animation phases
Read current frame to choose animation branch and pass a frame flag to spawned helpers
Set visual frame for the lift-phase animation
Prepare iteration over the tile stack before popping/moving entries
Remove/return stack entries at computed positions (often z+offset) as part of lift movement
Obtain ethereal layer top z to compute intermediate placement for moving items
Cause qualifying items to enter a falling state so they drop as part of the sink effect
Spawn a small visual/effect process (code 8) during the lift-phase
Spawn a small visual/effect process (code 5) during the complementary phase

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements a multi-stage "sinking" stepping-stone tile: when an actor enters its fast/trigger area it begins a timed sink animation that changes tile frames/types, plays SFX, spawns visual/effect helpers, and moves items between z-layers (lifting some up and letting others fall).
